english: Let’s go to the library where we went yesterday. ー Yes, let’s.
japanese: 昨日行った図書館に行こうよ。―うん、行こう。

english: You will be able to know the story when she comes back.
japanese: 彼女が帰ってきたら（帰ってきたとき）、君はその話を知ることができるだろう。

english: What is the subject (Which/that) your mother liked (the) best when she was a student? ー It is geography.
japanese: あなたのお母さんが学生時代に1番好きだった科目はなんですか？ー地理です。

english: Can (May)I let her read the book when it arrives? – Yes, you can (may).
japanese: その本が届いたら（届くとき）、彼女に読ませてもいいですか？

english: The book (which/that) her father wrote will be read in many countries.
japanese: 彼女のお父さんが書いた本は多くの国で読まれているだろう。

english: The clock (which/that) you saw has been at my home since I was a child.
japanese: 君が見た時計は、僕が子供の頃から我が家にいます。

english: How pretty the baby (whom/that) I met at the park was!
japanese: 公園であった赤ちゃんはなんてかわいかったんだろう！

english: I wonder what she wanted me to do.
japanese: 彼女は僕に何をして欲しかったんだろう。(wonderを使って)

english: Who came to the office while I was away (out)?
japanese: 僕の留守中に、誰かオフィスに来ましたか？

english: Have you watched the movie (which/that) I lent (to) you last week yet? ー No, I haven’t.
japanese: 私があなたに先週貸した映画をもう見ましたか？ーいえ、まだです。

english: As he was hungry, he asked his mother to make lunch.
japanese: お腹がすいていたので、彼はお母さんに昼食を作るように頼んだ。

english: There was a beautiful castle in the town where she was born.
japanese: 彼女の生まれた町には綺麗なお城があった。

english: The man (whom/that) I met yesterday was much taller than him [he is].
japanese: 私がきのう会った男の人は彼よりずっと背が高かった。

english: As there were many people (whom/that) she knew in the room, she kept smiling.
japanese: 部屋の中には彼女の知っている人がたくさんいたので、彼女は笑い続けていました。

english: How many pieces of bread did the man (who/that) you saw at the restaurant eat?
japanese: あなたがそのレストランで見た男の人は、パンをいくつ食べたのですか？

english: You don’t understand why she is loved by many people, do you?
japanese: あなたは、彼女がなぜ多くの人に愛されているのか、わからないのですね？

english: The student who came from Canada to make friends has been studying Japanese at this school since I first met him.
japanese: 友達を作るためにカナダから来たその学生は、私が彼に初めて会った時からずっとこの学校で日本語を勉強している。

english: She wanted her boyfriend to notice (that) she had had [got] a hair cut.
japanese: 彼女は恋人に自分が髪を切ったことを気づいて欲しかった。

english: He told his wife not to let their son sleep on the couch.
japanese: 彼は妻に、息子をソファーで寝かせるなと言った。

english: Will the children who grew up in a city be surprised to see mountains?
japanese: 都会で育ったその子供たちは山を見て驚くだろうか？

english: The language which [that] George is studying now is not spoken by very many people.
japanese: ジョージが今勉強している言語はあまり多くの人に話されていない。

english: The musician whose name is known to everyone will come to this town next month to hold a concert.
japanese: その名がみんなに知られている音楽家がコンサートを開くため来月この町に来る。

english: Have you ever been to a house which [that] is made of brick? / Have you ever been to a house made of brick? – Yes, I have.
japanese: あなたはレンガでできた家に行ったことありますか？ーはい、あります。

english: Although I wanted her to draw a picture, he made his sister do it.
japanese: 僕は彼女に絵を描いて欲しかったのだが、彼は妹にそれをやらせた。

english: I want you to tell me why John is in a good mood [humor].
japanese: 僕は君にジョンがなぜ機嫌がいいのか教えて欲しい。

english: When I saw her this morning, she said that she was going to climb a mountain.
japanese: 今朝彼女に会ったとき、彼女は山に登りに行くところだ、と言いました。

english: The rain made us give up going out.
japanese: 雨のため我々は外出するのを諦めなければならなかった。

english: I wonder if taking a walk before breakfast keeps her healthy.
japanese: 朝食前に散歩するので彼女は健康なのかしら？

english: As he is very smart, he will be able to solve a question (that/which) you can’t.
japanese: 彼はとても頭がいいので、君には分からない問題も解けるだろう。

english: She doesn’t think (that) it is necessary to go there.
japanese: 彼女は、そこに行くことが必要だとは思わない。

english: He spoke to a woman (who was) walking on the street to ask her where the station was.
japanese: 彼は駅がどこにあるか聞くために道を歩いている女性に話しかけた。

english: The girl whose mother was a teacher decided to study education at university.
japanese: お母さんが先生のその少女は大学で教育学を学ぶことを決めた。

english: Which is the house where [in which] he lives? ー That one (is).
japanese: どちらが、彼が住んでいる家ですか？ーあれです。

english: Although he went to France to learn French, his French is worse than mine.
japanese: 彼はフランス語を覚えるためにフランスに行ったけれど、彼のフランス語は私の（フランス語）より下手だ。

english: I want you to lend me the novel when you finish reading it.
japanese: 君がその小説を読み終わったら（終わったとき）、僕に貸してほしい。

english: Have you already sold the bag (which/that) you bought only last month?
japanese: 君は先月買ったばかりのカバンをもう売ってしまったのかい？

english: It will be very difficult [hard] to make her do this job.
japanese: 彼女にこの仕事をやらせることはとても難しいだろう。

english: When he got up in the morning, he felt disappointed to find (that) it was rainy.
japanese: 彼は朝起きたとき、天気が悪いことを知って(見つけて)がっかりした。

english: The watch (which/that) he broke has just been fixed.
japanese: 彼が壊した時計がちょうど直されたところです。

english: It is hard for her who stays up late every day to get up early in the morning.
japanese: 毎日夜更かしする彼女にとって、朝早く起きるのは辛い。

english: There were several accidents on this road on the day when it rained a lot.
japanese: 雨がたくさん降ったその日、この道でいくつか事故が起こりました。

english: Will the children who have never seen giraffes be surprised when they go to the zoo?
japanese: キリンを見たことがないその子供たちは動物園に行ったら驚くでしょうか？

english: When she was asked how old she was, she answered that she was thirty although [though] she was actually forty.
japanese: 何歳なのか聞かれたとき、実は40歳なのに、彼女は30歳だと答えた。

english: As he [she] didn’t know how to cook, the child had to wait for his [her] mother to come home.
japanese: どうやって料理をするのかわからなかったので、その子はお母さんが帰ってくるのを待たなければならなかった。

english: The boy who [that] wanted to get a good mark on a test than anybody else in the class studied till late at night.
japanese: クラスの他の誰よりもテストでいい点を取りたいと思っているその少年は、夜遅くまで勉強した。

english: Although [though] her mother wants her to be [become] a doctor, she has wanted to be a teacher since she was a child.
japanese: 彼女の母親は彼女に医者になって欲しいと思っているが、彼女は子供の頃からずっと先生になりたいと思っている。

english: The coffee (that/which) my mother made smells very good.
japanese: お母さんが作ったコーヒーはとても良い匂いがする。

english: When he was asked why he came home so late by his wife, he answered that he went to watch a baseball game with his colleagues.
japanese: 妻にどうしてそんなに遅く帰宅したのか聞かれた時、彼は同僚たちと野球観戦に行ったのだと答えた。

english: The foreigner who came to Canada only last month asked me to give him [her] English lessons.
japanese: 先月カナダに来たばかりのその外国人は私に英語のレッスンをしてくれと頼んだ。

english: Finally, they decided to let their daughter marry the guy [whom/that] she had been going out with for six years.
japanese: ついに彼らは娘を6年間付き合っているその男と結婚させてやることに決めた。

english: A man whom I have known for over twenty years is going to quit the company [which/that] he now works for next month to change jobs.
japanese: 私の20年来の知り合いである男性は、転職のために来月現在勤めている会社を辞める予定だ。

english: A friend of mine whose cousin lives in Germany says that he is going to go there this winter with his brother.
japanese: いとこがドイツに住んでいる僕の友人は、今年の冬弟と共にドイツに行くつもりだと言っている。

english: Have you ever met a girl who [that] cooks better than Satoko?
japanese: さとこより料理が上手な少女に会ったことがありますか？

english: The boy who was asked what he was doing by a teacher began to run as fast as he could [possible].
japanese: 教師に何をしているのかと尋ねられたその少年はできる限り速く走り始めた。

english: The child whose parents worked played in a park to kill time after school.
japanese: 両親が働いているその子供は放課後時間をつぶすために公園で遊んだ。

english: When I told my mother that the curry (which/that) she had made was too spicy for me to eat, she said that she had never heard anybody say such a rude thing.
japanese: お母さんに、お母さんが作ったカレーが辛すぎて僕は食べられないと言うと、お母さんは誰かがそんなに失礼なことを言うのを聞いたことがないと言った。

english: The weather forecast says that it will be fine today in the town where it snowed yesterday.
japanese: 昨日雪が降ったその町では、今日は晴れるだろうと、天気予報は言っている。

english: The park around which I run in the morning is visited by many people on holidays.
japanese: 私が朝周囲を走っている公園は、休日には多くの人に訪れられる。

english: Didn’t your father tell you to keep the window closed [shut] while you were playing the violin?
japanese: お父さんは(あなたが)バイオリンを弾いている間、窓を閉めておくようにとあなたに言いませんでしたか？

english: When his mother told him to keep his room tidy, he asked her why she didn’t tell his sister to do so.
japanese: 母親が彼に部屋をきれいにしておきなさいと命じたとき、彼女はなぜ妹にはそうしろと言わないのかと彼女に尋ねた。
